conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, let me read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me start by asking you some questions to assess your eligibility.

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex, he's 9 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 9 years old.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

**Are you responsible for Alex - meaning you provide for his care, such as food, clothes, and other necessities?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I take care of all his needs - food, clothes, school things, everything really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, that's very helpful.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

**Is Alex currently in hospital, in care, or in any residential accommodation, or has he been for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in hospital or care or anything like that. He's just at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you.

Just one final question to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex in approved education or training? (For example, does he attend school regularly?)**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he goes to school regularly - he's in primary school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news!** Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why you qualify:
- Alex is 9 years old (under 16)
- You live in the UK
- Alex lives with you at home
- You are responsible for his care and provide for all his needs
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- He's attending school regularly

You can now proceed to apply for Child Benefit. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, so as long as no one else claims for Alex, you should be able to receive the benefit.

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old, which is under 16, so he falls within the age criteria for Child Benefit. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and Alex lives with them at home. The claimant is responsible for Alex's care, providing for all his needs including food, clothes, and school supplies. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Alex attends primary school regularly, which constitutes approved education. According to the eligibility rules, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK, and they'll usually be responsible for a child if they live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 9), and meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. They live in the UK, Alex lives with them, they are responsible for Alex's care and upkeep, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and Alex is not in hospital or care. As Alex is under 16 and attending school, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

They live in the UK, Alex lives with them, they are responsible for Alex's care and upkeep, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and Alex is not in hospital or care. As Alex is under 16 and attending school, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.
